blueprint_refs: baseline_poi_choice_model: mixture of (a) empirical POI popularity ... and (b) distance-decay ...
poi-recall-near-zero-personal-preference.error_identification: POI top-k recall is essentially zero, meaning simulated days almost never include any ground-truth POIs even as a set match.
poi-recall-near-zero-personal-preference.root_cause_analysis: POI sampling within a super-category uses per-user POI preference weights (baseline.poi_pref) plus distance, but preference mass is extremely sparse (only on POIs seen in 2019–2020). With w_pref often high (best params ~0.94), the sampler concentrates on a small set of historically seen POIs, which in 2021 often differs from the user’s actual POIs; simultaneously, candidates per supercat are huge (all POIs in that supercat), and distance decay is computed from a context POI that may not be aligned to the day’s actual starting anchor, further reducing overlap with ground truth. Net: almost no exact token matches.
poi-recall-near-zero-personal-preference.correct_approach:
Make the POI-choice model less brittle and more user-anchored:
1) In Resident.simulate_day, define candidates as a union of (a) supercat candidates and (b) user-specific top-M POIs within that supercat (from baseline counts) plus (c) global top-M POIs within that supercat; then sample from this reduced candidate set.
2) Smooth poi_pref within a supercat: if a candidate is unseen by the user, give it a small prior proportional to global popularity (already computed in build_network_and_agents as global_poi_counts; reuse by exposing a per-supercat global popularity table rather than only a global list).
3) Lower default w_pref (e.g., 0.3–0.7) and widen calibration search; current best pushes it to ~0.94, reinforcing sparsity.
4) Ensure a consistent “start anchor” for distance: if ctx_poi exists but is not in the POI catalog / lacks coords, fall back to a home/anchor POI with coords.
poi-recall-near-zero-personal-preference.key_insight: When the evaluation requires exact POI token matches, any purely “user-history-only” POI preference will collapse recall under distribution shift; always include global/popularity smoothing and candidate-set pruning.

blueprint_refs: definition: error between simulated and GT daily radius of gyration computed from POI coordinates

evidence.metrics: No radius_of_gyration_error reported; instead trip_distance_wasserstein is used

missing-radius-of-gyration-metric-not-im.error_identification: Blueprint requires radius_of_gyration_error, but the implementation reports trip_distance_wasserstein and never computes radius of gyration.
missing-radius-of-gyration-metric-not-im.root_cause_analysis: Evaluator.compute_metrics computes step-distance Wasserstein (trip_distance_wasserstein) based on consecutive-visit distances, but does not compute per-day radius of gyration from POI coordinates nor compare to GT as specified.
missing-radius-of-gyration-metric-not-im.correct_approach:
Replace or add the blueprint metric:
- For each (user, day), map each visited POI to (lat, lon); drop visits with missing coords (as blueprint allows).
- Compute center of mass and radius of gyration r_g = sqrt(mean(||x_i - x_cm||^2)).
- Compute absolute error |r_g_sim - r_g_gt| (or relative if desired; blueprint allows absolute/relative). Aggregate mean over days.
- Emit as radius_of_gyration_error and (if keeping trip_distance_wasserstein for diagnostics) mark it non-objective or extra.
missing-radius-of-gyration-metric-not-im.key_insight: Don’t silently substitute evaluation metrics: implement blueprint metrics directly, and only add extras as explicitly non-contract diagnostics.

blueprint_refs: time_of_day_emd definition: Earth Mover's Distance over minutes-of-day
time-of-day-metric-uses-jsd-not-emd.error_identification: The simulator evaluates time-of-day distribution mismatch using JSD over coarse 30-min bins rather than the blueprint-required EMD over minutes-of-day.
time-of-day-metric-uses-jsd-not-emd.root_cause_analysis: Evaluator.compute_metrics builds weekday/weekend histograms on 30-min bins and computes js_divergence, outputting tod_jsd_avg. This deviates from the blueprint’s Earth Mover’s Distance requirement, and may hide systematic shifts (EMD is sensitive to “how far” mass moved).
time-of-day-metric-uses-jsd-not-emd.correct_approach:
Implement EMD (1D Wasserstein) on minute-of-day:
- Collect all visit minutes-of-day for GT and sim across the split (or per day then average).
- Compute wasserstein_1d on the raw minute lists (already implemented) or on cumulative distributions.
- Either replace tod_jsd_avg with time_of_day_emd or output both and align the objective to blueprint time_of_day_emd.
time-of-day-metric-uses-jsd-not-emd.key_insight: For circular/ordered domains like time-of-day, prefer distance-aware metrics (EMD/Wasserstein) over divergence on coarse bins when the spec demands shift magnitude.

blueprint_refs: category_mix_jsd definition: JSD between simulated vs ground-truth distributions of fine categories (or super-categories)
category-metric-computes-supercategory-s.error_identification: The implementation reports category_share_mae (mean absolute share difference) on super-categories, not JSD on categories/super-categories as specified.
category-metric-computes-supercategory-s.root_cause_analysis: Evaluator.compute_metrics maps each visit to a super-category and uses shares_mae; it never computes Jensen–Shannon divergence over fine categories or over super-categories.
category-metric-computes-supercategory-s.correct_approach:
Compute category_mix_jsd per blueprint:
- Decide whether to use fine category or super-category consistently (blueprint allows either; pick super-category if catto coverage is partial).
- Build normalized distributions for GT and sim across the split.
- Compute js_divergence and output as category_mix_jsd. Keep category_share_mae only as an auxiliary diagnostic if desired.
category-metric-computes-supercategory-s.key_insight: Match the metric family in the spec: MAE on shares and JSD capture different failures; using the wrong one can miscalibrate the shift parameters.

evidence.metrics: stop_count_abs_mean_error=2.2225; diagnostics gt_mean_visits_per_day=7.759 vs sim_mean_visits_per_day=6.686; stop_count_kl=0.4452

stop-count-model-underestimates-and-misa.error_identification: Stop counts are materially off: simulations average ~1.07 fewer visits/day than ground truth, with large per-day absolute error (~2.22) and distribution mismatch (KL~0.45).
stop-count-model-underestimates-and-misa.root_cause_analysis: Stop-count generation rescales a discrete pmf by rounding counts (c2 = round(c*mult)) and then applies ad-hoc neighbor smoothing (adding mass to c±1). This can distort the original distribution (multi-modal shapes collapse), and the later truncation in simulate_day (break if t_min >= day) further reduces realized stop count, biasing downward vs the sampled stop_count.
stop-count-model-underestimates-and-misa.correct_approach:
Stabilize stop-count control and prevent truncation bias:
1) Sample stop_count directly from the baseline pmf, then apply multiplier by shifting probabilities rather than rounding counts (e.g., reweight each count c by exp(-|c - c*mult|/tau) and renormalize).
2) Track when the time loop truncates (t_min>=MINUTES_PER_DAY) and either (a) cap gap sampling to fit remaining day time, or (b) allow later-day timestamps up to 23:59 by compressing gaps.
3) Add a diagnostic: sampled_stop_count vs realized_len(visits) to quantify truncation-induced error per split.
stop-count-model-underestimates-and-misa.key_insight: If you sample a target sequence length but later time-feasibility truncates the sequence, you must either enforce feasibility during length sampling or adjust gaps to honor the sampled length; otherwise stop-count metrics will be systematically biased.

blueprint_refs: radius_of_gyration_error requires POI lat/lon join; handle missing POIs by excluding those visits
POI catalog join by matching POI identifier strings
gt-coordinate-missingness-very-high-unde.error_identification: About 70% of ground-truth step distances cannot be computed due to missing coordinates, which makes distance-based evaluation noisy and potentially misleading.
gt-coordinate-missingness-very-high-unde.root_cause_analysis: The POI coordinate join is token-exact: dataset visit tokens must match poi_category_... tokens exactly. Any formatting differences (extra whitespace, punctuation, category name variations, casing) will cause a miss and be treated as missing coords. The simulator’s generated POIs all come from the POI catalog, so sim missingness is 0, creating an asymmetry.
gt-coordinate-missingness-very-high-unde.correct_approach:
Improve token normalization at parse/join time without inventing new files:
- In parse_1921y_records, normalize loc token by stripping trailing punctuation and collapsing internal whitespace.
- When loading poi_catalog, also store a normalized-key index (e.g., strip and collapse whitespace) to map GT tokens to POIInfo when exact match fails.
- Log top-N unmatched GT tokens to confirm whether misses are due to formatting vs truly absent POIs.
gt-coordinate-missingness-very-high-unde.key_insight: Distance-based metrics are only as good as the POI-token join; always implement robust normalization and log unmatched identifiers before trusting mobility-radius results.

blueprint_refs: Online OOD context window rule: construct a 7-day context window using only days strictly earlier than target; may backfill from late 2020

evidence.metrics: No direct metric; but affects realism and downstream recall/distance plausibility

context-window-rule-violated-by-unbounde.error_identification: If no visits exist in the strict 7-day window, the code backfills from any earlier day (potentially far earlier than late 2020), which can leak stale context and violate the intended 7-day rule semantics.
context-window-rule-violated-by-unbounde.root_cause_analysis: MobilitySimulator._context_last_poi first tries [target-7d, target), but on empty it falls back to any r.date < target_date without limiting how far back it goes. Blueprint permits backfill from late 2020, not arbitrary history.
context-window-rule-violated-by-unbounde.correct_approach:
Constrain the fallback:
- If the 7-day window is empty, extend the window backwards only to a bounded horizon (e.g., 30 days) and allow crossing year boundary into late 2020, but do not use arbitrarily old records.
- Alternatively, if no context in bounded window, fall back to anchor/home POI rather than ancient last visit.
context-window-rule-violated-by-unbounde.key_insight: Context windows in OOD simulations must be time-bounded even in fallback paths; otherwise you silently change the conditioning regime and may inflate/deflate metrics unpredictably.

blueprint_refs: comparison_method: evaluate on validation for calibration, then report held-out test once

evidence.metrics: evaluation_results_on_validation.json contains split=per_user_last_20pct_of_2021 and note says held-out V_test

output-contract-mismatch-evaluates-test-.error_identification: The program writes evaluation results on the per-user last-20% (test split) into a file named evaluation_results_on_validation.json, which is semantically confusing versus the blueprint naming.
output-contract-mismatch-evaluates-test-.root_cause_analysis: In main(), after calibrating on V_calib, it rolls out on splits.test_days_by_user but stores it under evaluation_results_on_validation and simulated_trajectories_validation.
output-contract-mismatch-evaluates-test-.correct_approach:
Align naming to split semantics while respecting required output filenames:
- Keep required filename evaluation_results_on_validation.json if mandated by external contract, but include explicit fields: {"evaluated_split": "V_test"} and optionally also write a separate evaluation_results_on_test.json if allowed by the environment (only if not prohibited).
- Alternatively, evaluate on V_calib and store that as “on_validation”, and store V_test metrics under calibrated_parameters.meta or another required artifact, ensuring no tuning uses V_test.
output-contract-mismatch-evaluates-test-.key_insight: When output filenames are fixed by contract, make the evaluated split unambiguous in the JSON payload to prevent accidental ‘validation vs test’ misuse downstream.
